turns.text: Q4 fiscal 2025 was our best quarter ever. Total cloud revenue was $7.0 billion, up 27% in constant currency. OCI revenue grew 51% year-over-year, our fastest growth in six quarters. We delivered fiscal year 2025 cloud revenue of $25.6 billion — exceeding our $25 billion commitment. For fiscal year 2026, we are highly confident in delivering $48 billion in cloud revenue, representing 87% growth. AI contract backlog now stands at $12.5 billion.

OCI capacity is doubling every year. We are building five new sovereign AI regions in fiscal 2026 in addition to the eight we opened in fiscal 2025. Our GenAI agents platform, which runs enterprise AI workflows on OCI, now has 800 production customers. We expect agent revenue to become a meaningful contributor to cloud revenue in fiscal 2027 as customers move beyond pilot deployments.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.
Safra, $48 billion in fiscal 2026 implies roughly 87% growth. That's a bold target. What is the capacity constraint risk, and how much of that is already in contracted backlog?

statements.claim: Oracle delivered fiscal year 2025 cloud revenue of $25.6 billion, exceeding the $25 billion commitment.
OCI revenue grew 51% year-over-year in Q4 fiscal 2025.
Oracle is highly confident in delivering $48 billion in fiscal 2026 cloud revenue, representing 87% growth.
Oracle GenAI agents platform now has 800 production customers on OCI.
Oracle AI contract backlog stands at $12.5 billion.
